title: Cauliflower cheese
subtitle: Blanched florets in a sharp cheddar béchamel, baked until bubbling
excerpt: Cauliflower blanched until just tender, arranged in a dish, and covered with a sharp cheddar béchamel sauce, then baked until the surface is golden and bubbling. A British roast accompaniment and a standalone supper dish. The sauce must be properly seasoned and the cauliflower must not be overcooked before baking.
sourceNotes: Cauliflower cheese is one of the defining dishes of British home cooking — universally eaten, rarely described as interesting, and deeply satisfying when made properly. It appears as a Sunday roast accompaniment, a standalone main, and a Boxing Day side. The sauce is a standard béchamel made sharp with cheddar and mustard.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Assemble a day ahead and refrigerate unbaked. Add 10 minutes to the baking time from cold.

content.text: The cauliflower should be blanched until it offers just slight resistance when prodded with a knife — it will continue to cook in the oven. If blanched to full tenderness before baking, it will turn to mush in the dish. Err on the side of undercooking at this stage.

A properly seasoned cheese sauce is the whole dish. Use a well-aged, sharp cheddar. Add mustard to cut through the fat of the cheese — without it the sauce can taste flat. Season aggressively with salt and pepper; cauliflower absorbs a lot of seasoning.

items.prepNote: mature, grated, plus extra for the top

content.text: Bring a large pan of salted water to the boil. Add the {{cauliflower}} florets and cook for 4 minutes, until just starting to soften but still with a firm bite. Drain thoroughly and spread in a large baking dish.

Heat the oven to 200°C. Melt the {{unsalted-butter}} in a medium saucepan over medium heat. Add the {{plain-flour}} and cook, stirring, for 1 minute. Remove from the heat and gradually add the {{whole-milk}}, whisking constantly to prevent lumps. Return to the heat and stir continuously until the sauce thickens and bubbles. Remove from the heat. Add the {{cheddar}}, {{dijon-mustard}}, {{sea-salt-fine}}, and {{black-pepper}}. Stir until the cheese melts. Pour over the cauliflower. Scatter extra grated cheddar over the top. Bake for 20 to 25 minutes, until the surface is golden and bubbling at the edges.

items.cause: Milk added too quickly or all at once
items.fix: Add the milk off the heat, in small amounts at first, whisking constantly. Once half is incorporated the sauce is stable enough to add the rest in a steady stream

items.symptom: Watery liquid pooling under the sauce after baking
items.fix: After blanching, drain and then spread on a clean tea towel for a few minutes to remove excess water before placing in the baking dish
Use mature or extra-mature cheddar. The mustard is not optional for flavour. Taste the sauce before pouring and season assertively

content.text: Cauliflower cheese is eaten as a Sunday roast side alongside beef or lamb, as a standalone supper with crusty bread, or as a Boxing Day accompaniment to cold cuts. It is one of the few dishes where the name describes the method exactly: cauliflower, in cheese sauce.
